name: title-subtitle
text: Harnessing abundance, plurality and divergence in AI translation of ancient texts
textPreview: Harnessing abundance, plurality and divergence in AI translation of ancient texts

name: title-authors
text: Greg Baker | Shirley Chan | Vanessa Enriquez Raido | Greta Hawes
textPreview: Greg Baker | Shirley Chan | Vanessa Enriquez Raido | Greta Hawes

name: title-venue
text:
AI4AS 2026, online mini-conference within DH2026
27 July 2026 | Daejeon, South Korea / remote
textPreview: AI4AS 2026, online mini-conference within DH2026 | 27 July 2026 | Daejeon, South Korea / remote

name: title-claim-body
text: Human judgement shows a directional raw signal, but length explains enough that the responsible claim is methodological rather than conclusive.
textPreview: Human judgement shows a directional raw signal, but length explains enough that the responsible claim is methodological rather than concl...

name: title-footer
text: Published abstract: theoretical framework, corpus rationale, preliminary pilot findings
textPreview: Published abstract: theoretical framework, corpus rationale, preliminary pilot findings

text:
Open by locating the talk: this is the AI4AS 2026 mini-conference on ancient scripts, held online on 27 July 2026.
The submitted abstract commits us to theory, corpus rationale, and preliminary pilot findings. Vanessa's first-set review gives us the pilot result we can responsibly discuss.

name: title-2
text: A single fluent translation hides a plural process
textPreview: A single fluent translation hides a plural process

name: single-output-body
text:
one clean English surface
one implied authority
one path through ambiguity
textPreview: one clean English surface | one implied authority | one path through ambiguity

name: ancient-risk-body
text:
low-resource uncertainty
fragmentary evidence
cultural specificity
into fluent confidence
textPreview: low-resource uncertainty | fragmentary evidence | cultural specificity | into fluent confidence

name: problem-close
text: The problem is not abundance. The problem is unstructured abundance.
textPreview: The problem is not abundance. The problem is unstructured abundance.

text:
The abstract begins from translation as situated and purposive, not a lookup task.
Use this slide to set up why one fluent model output is risky for ancient-language work: it removes signs of choice just where the reader most needs them.

name: title-3
text: Parallage turns AI abundance into inspectable evidence
textPreview: Parallage turns AI abundance into inspectable evidence

name: pack-label
text: A translation pack is not a vote. It is a structured field of alternatives.
textPreview: A translation pack is not a vote. It is a structured field of alternatives.

name: pack-cues
text: Inspection cues: source-segment alignment | named entities | ambiguity points | differences that matter
textPreview: Inspection cues: source-segment alignment | named entities | ambiguity points | differences that matter

text:
This is the core methodological claim. Parallage is not an attempt to make one better AI translation.
It structures model abundance into a pack that makes interpretive choice visible: literal, readable, interpretive, uncertainty-marked, and adversarial renderings with cues for inspection.

name: title-4
text: The corpus makes reliability hard to outsource
textPreview: The corpus makes reliability hard to outsource

name: greek-bullets
text:
- geographical lexicon
- compressed source tradition
- named entities carry the argument
- pilot analysis available now
textPreview: - geographical lexicon | - compressed source tradition | - named entities carry the argument | - pilot analysis available now

name: chinese-bullets
text:
- Classical Chinese geography
- dense administrative and cultural categories
- tests the same method outside Greek
- evaluation stream still to complete
textPreview: - Classical Chinese geography | - dense administrative and cultural categories | - tests the same method outside Greek | - evaluation str...

name: corpus-conclusion
text: Neither case lets a non-source-language reader simply defer to a stable complete English translation.
textPreview: Neither case lets a non-source-language reader simply defer to a stable complete English translation.

text:
The abstract names two corpora: Stephanos and Hanshu Dilizhi.
Make the rationale explicit: these are not just examples; they are contexts where trust and agency become visible because the reader cannot rely on a finished English canon.

name: title-5
text: The first pilot asks whether expert judgement tracks overlap fragility
textPreview: The first pilot asks whether expert judgement tracks overlap fragility

name: pilot-bullets
text:
- Vanessa rated expected divergence from hidden human translations.
- We compared those ratings with sentence-aligned overlap metrics.
- Then we adjusted scores for reference length, because all three metrics worsen on longer documents.
textPreview: - Vanessa rated expected divergence from hidden human translations. | - We compared those ratings with sentence-aligned overlap metrics. ...

text:
Explain the design carefully. Vanessa's rating is human judgement about expected divergence.
The automatic scores compare focal machine outputs against approved hidden human translations. They are diagnostic proxies, not ground truth for translation quality.

name: title-6
text: Raw overlap gives a directional but fragile signal
textPreview: Raw overlap gives a directional but fragile signal

name: raw-readout-title
text:
Spearman r
rating vs raw score
textPreview: Spearman r | rating vs raw score

name: raw-readout-note
text: Negative is the expected direction: higher human divergence rating, lower lexical overlap.
textPreview: Negative is the expected direction: higher human divergence rating, lower lexical overlap.

alt: Raw metric scatter plots against Vanessa rating

text:
This is the answer to the first version of the question: yes, weakly and directionally, Vanessa was often pointing toward lower overlap cases.
But the correlations are modest and the sample is only ten. Treat this as a raw signal, not the conclusion.

name: title-7
text: Length is a real confound, not a footnote
textPreview: Length is a real confound, not a footnote

name: length-caption
text: Set 1 spans 18-268 reference words, and Vanessa's ratings also rise with length.
textPreview: Set 1 spans 18-268 reference words, and Vanessa's ratings also rise with length.

alt: Length and composite residual scatter plot

text:
All of the overlap metrics do worse on longer documents in the broader population.
That matters because Set 1 is small and Vanessa's ratings also rise with length. If we do not control for length, we overstate what the raw metrics are telling us.

name: title-8
text: After length adjustment, the signal becomes cautious
textPreview: After length adjustment, the signal becomes cautious

name: adjusted-readout-title
text:
Spearman r
rating vs residual badness
textPreview: Spearman r | rating vs residual badness

name: adjusted-readout-note
text: The adjusted direction is positive, but weak at n=10. This is preliminary evidence for workflow value, not proof.
textPreview: The adjusted direction is positive, but weak at n=10. This is preliminary evidence for workflow value, not proof.

alt: Length-adjusted residual scatter plots against Vanessa rating

text:
This answers Greg's follow-up question. Once we account for length, there is still a hint of signal, especially on 3-gram F1 residual badness, but it is weak and not robust.
The right claim is that the review task is producing analyzable judgement data and that the disagreements are diagnostically useful.

name: title-9
text: The disagreements are the result, not a nuisance
textPreview: The disagreements are the result, not a nuisance

name: mismatch-list
text:
- Karia: high rating, length explains much of the low score
- Kadmeia: low rating, poor adjusted overlap
- Kavalis: high rating, strong overlap
textPreview: - Karia: high rating, length explains much of the low score | - Kadmeia: low rating, poor adjusted overlap | - Kavalis: high rating, stro...

name: diagnosis-line
text: These are exactly the cases a pack-based interface should surface for review.
textPreview: These are exactly the cases a pack-based interface should surface for review.

text:
Use this slide to avoid sounding apologetic about the weak adjusted correlations.
For Parallage, the mismatch between human judgement, overlap metrics, and document length is itself useful: it identifies where a reader or reviewer needs better evidence.

name: title-10
text: What we can responsibly claim at AI4AS
textPreview: What we can responsibly claim at AI4AS

name: claim-one-body
text: Parallage gives AI translation abundance a visible, auditable structure.
textPreview: Parallage gives AI translation abundance a visible, auditable structure.

name: claim-two-body
text: Vanessa's first set shows a raw signal, a length confound, and weak residual signal.
textPreview: Vanessa's first set shows a raw signal, a length confound, and weak residual signal.

name: claim-three-body
text: Scale to more reviewers, the Chinese stream, and direct comparison with single-output translation.
textPreview: Scale to more reviewers, the Chinese stream, and direct comparison with single-output translation.

name: claim-close
text: The evidence supports a publishable method-in-progress: not proof that the tool is better, but proof that the judgement problem is measurable.
textPreview: The evidence supports a publishable method-in-progress: not proof that the tool is better, but proof that the judgement problem is measur...

text:
End with the strongest responsible claim.
Do not claim the pilot proves Parallage improves reliability. Claim that Parallage turns multiplicity into a structured object for judgement, and that the first pilot shows how the judgement data can be analyzed.

name: source-list
text:
- AI4AS 2026 programme: the talk is in the 11:00-12:00 AI and Translations session on 27 July 2026.
- Published abstract: Baker, Chan, Enriquez Raido, Hawes, Into the Parallage.
- Macquarie project page: Into the Parallage: harnessing AI abundance in translation tasks.
- Variantum local analysis: Vanessa Set 1 scatter data and generated metric plots.
textPreview: - AI4AS 2026 programme: the talk is in the 11:00-12:00 AI and Translations session on 27 July 2026. | - Published abstract: Baker, Chan, ...

text:
Keep this slide as backup rather than presenting it unless questions come up.
It makes the deck self-contained and records where the abstract and pilot evidence came from.
